title: Protein expression in Komagataella phaffii (formerly Pichia pastoris)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"ahal9","text":"The methylotrophic yeast Komagataella phaffii (formerly Pichia pastoris) is the most commonly used yeast species in the production of recombinant proteins. This is most likely due to its ability to grow to high cell density, to express recombinant genes in a tightly controlled manner and to efficiently secrete proteins. Despite its biotechnological importance and wide use in industry, relatively few genetic tools are readily available for academic research. Here, we present a protocol for production of proteins in K. phaffii.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":25,"length":20},{"style":"italic","offset":56,"length":15},{"style":"italic","offset":520,"length":11}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a3977","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ak1pj","text":"The protocol found here is modified from the protocol provided in the Pichia Expression Kit (Catalog Number K1710-01, Invitrogen).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":70,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"dk3e8","text":"Transformation and integration into K. phaffii GS115 can be done utilizing the Pichia EasyComp Kit (Catalog no. K1730-01, Invitrogen).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":36,"length":11},{"style":"italic","offset":79,"length":6}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9bq0c","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2knec","text":"Recipes for all media and solutions can also be found in the manual for the Pichia Expression Kit (Catalog Number K1710-01, Invitrogen).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":76,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"4606v","text":"Make sure to have your expression strain streaked on an YPD agar plate","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"8orog","text":"This protocol describes the use of GMO classified organisms. Make sure that the local GMO and safety legislations are respected.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8n6vd","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"85nj1","text":"This protocol involves the use of 100 % methanol. steps: Prepare BMGY and BMMY media Ingredients (final concentrations): 1 Mass / % volume yeast extract 2 Mass / % volume peptone 100 millimolar (mM) mM potassium phosphate, pH 6.0 1.34 Mass / % volume YNB 0.0004 Mass / % volume biotin 1 % volume glycerol (BMGY) or 0.5 % volume methanol (BMMY) K. phaffii cells exhibit optimal growth with higher YNB concentrations than S. cerevisiae
Dissolve 10 g of yeast extract, 20 g of peptone in 700 mL of water
Autoclave for 00:20:00 on liquid a cycle program

Let cool down to room temperature, then add the following: 100 mL 1 Molarity (M) potassium phosphate buffer, pH 6 (autoclaved stock solution) 100 mL 13.4 Mass / % volume YNB with ammonium sulfate without amino acids (filter sterilized stock solution) 2 mL 0.02 Mass / % volume biotin (filter sterilized stock solution)
For BMGY add 100 mL 10 % volume glycerol For BMMY add 100 mL 5 % volume methanol If not used directly, store the media at 4 °C for up to 2 months
Prepare 1 Molarity (M) potassium phosphate buffer, pH 6
Transfer 2.5 mL of the saturated culture into 100 mL BMGY in a 500 mL shake flask with low baffles and grow overnight at 28 °C It is also possible to grow the cells at 30 °C
Measure OD 600 of the overnight culture
Pellet 50 mL of the culture in a 50 mL falcon tube at 4000 x g, Room temperature, 10 minutes
Inoculate 25 mL of BMGY in a 250 mL shake flask with a single colony of K. phaffii GS115 with the desired construct integrated on the genome.
Prepare 13.4 Mass / % volume YNB with ammonium sulfate without amino acids
Prepare 0.2 Mass / % volume biotin solution
Combine 132 mL of 1 Molarity (M) K 2 HPO 4 and 868 mL of 1 Molarity (M) KH 2 PO 4
Confirm that the pH 6.0 and adjust using phosphoric acid or KOH
Sterilize by autoclaving for 00:20:00 on liquid cycle program and store at Room temperature The shelf life of this solution is greater than one year

Dissolve 134 g of yeast nitrogen base (YNB) with ammonium sulfate and without amino acids in 1.000 L of water. The solution can be heated to dissolve YNB completely in water Alternatively, use 34 g of YNB without ammonium sulfate and amino acids and 100 g of ammonium sulfate.
Sterilize by filteration and store at 4 °C The shelf life of this solution is approximately one year
Dissolve 20 mg biotin in 100 mL of water
Sterilize by filteration and store at 4 °C The shelf life of this solution is approximately one year
Incubate the culture at 28 °C with 250RPM shaking until saturation It is also possible to grow the cells at 30 °C

Discard the supernatant and resuspend the pellet in BMMY medium to a final OD 600 of 50. The appropriate amount of media can be calculated as: V_{BMMY} = \frac{OD_{600,measured}\cdot V_{pre-culture}}{50}

Grow the cells at 28 °C and 250 RPM of shaking It is crucial that the temperature does not exceed 28 °C when producing protein. It is advised to double-check the incubator's temperature reading with a thermometer.
Add 1% methanol to the expression culture ( 1 mL of 100 % volume methanol to 100 mL of BMMY culture) around 24 hours after inoculation
Add 1% methanol to the expression culture ( 1 mL of 100 % volume methanol to 100 mL of BMMY culture) around 24 hours after last addition of methanol
Add 1% methanol to the expression culture ( 1 mL of 100 % volume methanol to 100 mL of BMMY culture) around 24 hours after last addition of methanol
Harvest the cells by centrifugation at 4000 x g, 4 °C, 10 minutes and discard the pellets
Keep the sample On ice when working with it and at 4 °C or -20 °C for storage
Inoculate 100 mL BMMY medium with 2 mL of the normalized pre-culture, giving a start OD 600 of 1.
creator: Maja Rennig
